title: net/url References, Escaping, Host Identity, and Conservative Keys · Language Lens: Escaped Path Policy
summary: Opaque and hierarchical URLs, RawPath, default ports, fragments, Unicode hosts, query encoding, and base resolution are reduced to unsafe string operations. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go escaped path policy increment in Go URL identity and scope admission pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theor…

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Predict Escaped Path Policy · Theory Escaped Path Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 2364 during a mixed-origin fairness and race rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile net/url structure, references, escaping, IDNA, host identity, and conservative keys; every URL, body, tree, record, frontier transition, and report row retains causal identity; then reorder one origin, retry, channel, context, goroutine, Colly callback, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
